article_meta.title: War in UkraineKremlin says leaked U.S

reasoning: Mostly neutral and attributive, with minor adoption of the article’s evaluative framing (e.g., stating the plan “heavily favored Moscow” without hedging and using interpretive phrasing like “walking back”). No sensational or ad hominem language; perspectives are broadly represented.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses mildly interpretive phrasing (“walking back,” “remained cautious”); otherwise neutral verbs and attributions.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Most claims are tied to reported leaks and quotes; the evaluative “heavily favored Moscow” is stated without attribution/hedging.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Presents the plan as “heavily favored Moscow” and “requires” certain concessions, dropping the article’s hedging (“appeared to”).
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming language beyond the article’s factual content.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about unspoken intentions or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Covers Kremlin, Trump/WH, Ukraine; notes Russia’s caution. No clear favoring of one side.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Minor: does not quote William Taylor’s criticism explicitly in the summary, though controversy is noted.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: “Heavily favored” and “cautious” are mildly subjective; rest is neutral.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Generally neutral; “abandon NATO” in key fact is slightly stronger than the article’s phrasing.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Tone aligns with source; no added negative emphasis.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official and media sources comparably; no deference imbalance.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Largely neutral with small instances of inherited evaluative framing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Most perspectives included; one critic’s view is summarized as “controversy” rather than quoted.
